title: Prompts and artifacts are data sinks
description: Model prompts, tool transcripts, screenshots, generated fixtures, and exported artifacts can persist personal data outside the application boundary.
body: Use when debugging with AI tools, preparing reports, generating tests from real data, or creating screenshots/log bundles. Do not put real personal data into prompts unless explicitly approved. Actions: use synthetic or redacted data, crop or mask screenshots, and avoid copying raw records into generated files. Validation signals include artifact inspection and redacted examples. Risk boundary: PHI, PCI, credentials, or customer records in prompts require human gate. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
